prompt: I'm designing the new canon.activity_runs table schema. We need columns for cost tracking, retry counts, and error categorization. Walk me through the column choices and propose the migration SQL.

assertions.name: skill-correctly-did-not-trigger
assertions.text: [with_skill only] The agent's report explicitly states the skill did NOT apply (out of scope per the description's 'Do NOT use for' list). The reasoning correctly identifies schema/data-model design as non-UI work.

assertions.name: no-wireframes-folder-created
assertions.text: The outputs do NOT contain a wireframes/ folder, a design-intent README for UI, or any HTML mockup files. The skill should not have produced any of its standard artifacts.

assertions.name: schema-design-delivered
assertions.text: Outputs contain a schema design — column list with justifications for cost_usd / retry_count / error_category (or equivalent names). Addresses all three concerns named in the user's prompt.

assertions.name: migration-sql-delivered
assertions.text: Outputs contain valid PostgreSQL migration SQL (CREATE TABLE or ALTER TABLE for canon.activity_runs) with the proposed columns. The SQL is syntactically reasonable (semicolons, valid column types).
